content:
- Completed the bug task: task file is marked done/passes true, acceptance criteria are checked, and Ralph task_switch has been run.
- task_switch refreshed current_tasks/current_tasks_done and sent the completion email.
- Next I am reviewing the final worktree, then I will add all files, commit with the required task-finished message including test evidence and the manual verification result, and push.

- Read prior codex progress, refreshed Ralph summaries, and applied the choose-task rules against current_tasks/current_tasks_done.
- Confirmed the actual stack from the repo: Go module `pg_gobench` with `cmd/pg_gobench`, `internal/*`, `make lint` using gofmt+go vet, and `make test` using go test ./....
- Verified the only active failing task is `.ralph/tasks/story-99-manual-verify-everything/task-01-manual-verify-everything.md`, and its only blocker `.ralph/tasks/story-09-ui/task-01-standalone-html-control-page.md` already has `<passes>true</passes>` in current_tasks_done, so story-99 is the next selectable task.
- Next step in a new context window is to execute story-99 manual verification end to end, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` remain green or file bugs immediately if they do not.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with the selected next task path for Ralph.
- Verified the selection points to the unblocked failing manual-verification task in story-99.
- Quit immediately after selection as required by the choose-task instructions.

- Read previous codex progress and confirmed the selected current task is story-99 manual verification.
- Loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions before touching the task.
- Next I am reading the task file and story context to decide whether this turn must create/update a plan and quit or execute the task directly.

- Read the story-99 task file and confirmed it already has a plan path and is a manual-verification-only task.
- Confirmed this task explicitly says not to use TDD and not to run make test/check/lint during the manual pass.
- Next I am reading the stored plan and boundary-smell guidance, then I will prepare the real verification environment and start exercising the product feature by feature.

- Read the manual verification plan and confirmed it ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Started execution by checking local runtime tooling (Docker, Compose, kubectl, browser availability) and rereading README/examples/workflow/UI artifacts.
- Next I will bring up the committed runtime surfaces and manually exercise the live API; if any real defect appears I will create a bug task immediately and switch.

- Confirmed Docker and Compose are installed; kubectl is present but with slightly different CLI flags; no standard browser binary is on PATH yet.
- Started the committed Compose example as the first live verification surface.
- Next I will verify health/readiness/API behavior against the live stack and then assess truthful fallbacks for browser and Kubernetes validation.

- Compose stack came up successfully with postgres healthy and pg_gobench started.
- Began baseline live checks against health, readiness, benchmark state/results, metrics, and service logs before starting any workload.
- Next I will drive benchmark start/alter/stop flows and then inspect schema/data behavior through PostgreSQL itself.

- Baseline HTTP and metrics checks passed: healthz/readyz returned 200, idle benchmark endpoints returned expected JSON shapes, and pg_gobench_ Prometheus metrics were present.
- Started control-plane verification with idle rejection cases and a short mixed workload run.
- Next I will inspect active-run state, alter behavior, single-active-run rejection, then query PostgreSQL to verify schema and scale effects.

- Verified a full mixed workload slice end to end: start/alter/state/results/metrics all updated, single-active-run rejection returned 409, and PostgreSQL showed the expected bench schema and scale-1 row counts.
- Before running the remaining profiles I am reading the public profile names and contention semantics from the code/docs so I can hit the real API contract rather than guessing.
- Next I will exercise transaction/join/aggregation/lock/hot-row workloads and inspect visible failure surfaces.

- Confirmed the advanced workload mapping from code: join drives both join+aggregation operations and lock drives lock_contention+hot_update operations.
- Started a short live profile sweep for read, write, transaction, join, and lock plus start-request rejection checks for invalid lock clients, invalid read_percent usage, and unknown JSON fields.
- Next I will review the results for surfaced errors/contention and then move into config-mode verification and runtime artifacts like the scratch image.

- Finished the workload sweep: read/write/transaction/join all produced the expected live operation families, and lock surfaced real contention errors with nonzero failed_operations and a PostgreSQL 55P03 latest_error.
- I am now reading the config loader and Dockerfile so I can verify credential modes and rejection paths through the real scratch image boundary.
- Next I will run temporary config variants for value/env-ref/secret-file success cases plus strict and TLS validation failures.

- Rebuilt the scratch image from the committed Dockerfile and verified it ran successfully with literal credentials plus a swapped secret-file/env-ref credential setup.
- Verified config rejection paths through the real image: multiple credential modes, unknown YAML field, missing env-ref, and missing TLS CA file all failed with the expected startup boundary errors.
- Next I am finding an honest browser path for the standalone HTML page and checking whether a local kind cluster is available for the Kubernetes slice.

- Verified the standalone HTML page through a real Chromium headless session opened from file://, including saved API URL persistence, health/ready/state/results/metrics actions, metrics popup launch, and live start/alter/stop flows.
- Rebuilt pg_gobench:local, loaded it into the local kind cluster, applied the committed Kubernetes manifests, and confirmed both postgres and pg-gobench deployments reached Available.
- Next I will port-forward the in-cluster service and verify the same health/readiness/control-plane behavior against the live ConfigMap/Secret-backed deployment.

- Kubernetes verification passed through the local kind cluster: the committed manifests applied as-is, the pg-gobench deployment showed ConfigMap and Secret mounts, and the in-cluster service answered health/ready/benchmark/results/metrics over a port-forward.
- I am now finishing the last manual checks for graceful shutdown on an explicit bind address and the locally observable release workflow/docs claims.
- Next I will either close the checklist and mark the task passing or stop immediately if one of these final truth checks exposes a real defect.

- Verified graceful shutdown on an explicit overridden bind address with the scratch image: health worked on the overridden port and docker stop led to exit code 0.
- Verified the public release surface where observable: the latest GitHub Actions publish workflow run succeeded on 2026-04-30 for commit c66a066..., and GHCR inspection for that SHA showed a multi-arch index with linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 manifests.
- No real product defects were found in the manual pass; next I am updating the task file to check off the completed manual verification items and mark the task passing before running Ralph closeout and git push.

- Updated the story-99 manual verification task file to status done, passes true, and checked every completed manual checklist/acceptance item.
- Started cleanup of the live verification environments: shutting down the Compose stack, deleting the Kubernetes resources, and closing the active port-forward.
- Next I will run Ralph task_switch, review the final git state, commit all tracked changes with the required task-finished message, and push.

- Compose and Kubernetes cleanup completed after the manual pass.
- Triggered Ralph task_switch as required now that the task is marked done/passes true.
- Next I will inspect the resulting git state, commit all repo changes with the required task-finished message including manual verification evidence, and push.
